text.title: Feature Prioritisation — RICE Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Feature Prioritisation — RICE. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Score = Reach × Impact × Confidence ÷ Effort. All 4 dimensions mandatory.
rule.rationale: Score = Reach × Impact × Confidence ÷ Effort. All 4 dimensions mandatory.

rule.statement: Items with confidence <50% require a spike before scoring.
rule.rationale: Items with confidence <50% require a spike before scoring.

rule.statement: Items with effort >2 person-weeks require split or spike before scoring.
rule.rationale: Items with effort >2 person-weeks require split or spike before scoring.

rule.statement: Reach unit (e.g. users/quarter) and effort unit (person-weeks) consistent across the batch.
rule.rationale: Reach unit (e.g. users/quarter) and effort unit (person-weeks) consistent across the batch.

rule.statement: Top item is captured as a decision record with the scoring table attached.
rule.rationale: Top item is captured as a decision record with the scoring table attached.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-feature-prioritization-rice.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
